task.title: Fix IME Enter sending message during composition (chat input)

messages.content:
Fix IME composition Enter bug in the S-Deck chat input.

Problem: When the operator uses a Chinese (or any IME) input method, pressing Enter to commit the candidate text submits the message instead of just committing the IME composition. This is a common keydown-vs-composition bug — the Enter keydown handler fires even though the keypress was meant for the IME, not the form.

Standard fix: check for `event.isComposing` (or `event.keyCode === 229`) on the keydown handler and bail out if true. The browser fires `compositionstart` / `compositionend` events; during composition, Enter should only commit the candidate, not submit.

Recent context — commit `5286eee fix(ui): restore Enter/Shift+Enter/Alt+Enter keybindings in chat input` likely added the keybinding code that needs the IME guard. Look there first.

Steps:
1. cd into .synapse/worktrees/developer-37-ime-composition-enter
2. Locate the chat input keydown handler (likely in public/app.js or similar). Look at commit 5286eee for the file.
3. Add the IME guard: skip submit if `e.isComposing || e.keyCode === 229`.
4. Test mentally: the same logic must apply to whatever modifier keys are bound (Enter submit, Shift+Enter newline, etc.).
5. Commit inside the worktree with a focused message: `fix(ui): skip Enter submit during IME composition`.
6. report_done with one-line summary; reference any nuances in a report file only if needed.

Worktree: .synapse/worktrees/developer-37-ime-composition-enter
Branch: synapse/developer-37-ime-composition-enter
Do not push. Orchestrator will merge after DONE.
